title: Windmannia auriculifera
parts: I couldn't find any information on the specific medicinal parts of the plant Windmannia auriculifera.
intro: Windmannia auriculifera, also known as Windmannia, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the family Gesneriaceae. Traditionally, Windmannia has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and digestive problems, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Windmannia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Windmannia is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional remedies such as decoctions and poultices.
